Psychology · Mental Health · Research

Kayla Park

I build tools that help young people feel heard — pairing psychology research with the everyday support teens actually reach for.

Founder of an anonymous peer-support app, contributor to public health policy research, award-winning writer, and cellist. A student leader using evidence and empathy to expand access to mental health care.

About

Psychology-focused student interested in mental health, human behavior, and access to care. I founded a youth-led peer-support initiative, research harm-reduction policy with a Johns Hopkins biostatistician, and connect writing and music to service — a consistent through-line of using evidence and empathy to support young people.

Leonia High School — Leonia, NJ · Expected June 2027
GPA 4.7 weighted / 3.84 unweighted · 5 AP courses incl. AP Psychology · National, Rho Kappa & Science Honor Societies

Research

Independent Public Health Policy Research2024 – Present
  • Conduct harm-reduction policy research alongside a Johns Hopkins biostatistician, applying spatial and quantitative analysis to questions of public health and human behavior.
  • Analyze block-level spatial data on NYC supervised consumption sites to identify neighborhood-level patterns in public drug use.
  • Connect findings to active policy debates on supervised consumption, linking quantitative evidence to real public health decisions.
Research poster — “Beyond the Facility Walls: A Geospatial Analysis of Supervised Consumption Sites and Neighborhood Effects,” co-presented with Eunice Cho.

Mental Health & Youth Advocacy

VOY Mental Health Initiative — Founder & President2024 – Present
  • Founded a student-led mental health initiative after identifying gaps in peer support, growing it into an ongoing program of wellness and stress-management resources.
  • Designed and launched Mo Soop (April 2026), an anonymous peer-support app where students share stress without judgment and find encouragement — modeled on the Korean idea of a safe “bamboo forest.”
  • Led a Finals Relief Project: student volunteers 3D-printed and donated 150 stress-relief clickers to Fort Lee High School during finals season.
  • Prototyped Mo Ri, a companion app for students with ADHD; now building study tools that help them learn core subjects, plus self-advocacy and emotional-support resources.
Open Mo Soop ↗
“Benched” — College Athlete Mental Health Documentary — Co-Creator & Mental Health Lead2025 – Present
  • Co-creating a short documentary on how NCAA athletes navigate identity, mental health, and the pull between athletic demands and life after sports.
  • Lead the film’s psychological framing and athlete outreach, shaping interviews and linking subjects to peer mental health resources.
  • Developing a companion policy brief on supporting student-athlete wellbeing, with plans to submit the film to student festivals.

Leadership & Community Service

Young Global Leaders of the East (YGLE) — President2023 – Present
  • Lead a student-run organization, directing volunteer, cultural, and youth-empowerment programming through the year.
  • Launched the organization’s first Global Leadership Forum, convening peers to examine youth social issues. Young Leadership Award (2025).
Clothing Donations to Help Kids in the World (CDHW) — President2023 – Present
  • Direct a student nonprofit that has distributed $15,000+ in clothing to hospitalized children and families in need.
  • Expanding the program’s reach to children in foster care nationwide. Outstanding Service Milestone, 200+ hours (2026).
Liberty in North Korea (LiNK) – North Jersey Rescue Team — Student Leader2023 – Present
  • Lead a student chapter advocating for North Korean refugees, authoring educational materials that build peer awareness of human rights.
  • Organize fundraising campaigns supporting refugee rescue and resettlement. Chapter Recognition Award (2025).
Walk Alongside Tutoring Program — Lead Mentor2022 – Present
  • Mentor underserved students weekly, pairing academic support with the confidence that keeps them engaged.
  • Recruit and coordinate volunteer tutors and build the curriculum they teach from. Tutor of the Year (2025).
In the community: delivering clothing to Hackensack Meridian Children’s Hospital, preparing CDHW donations, and mentoring with Walk Alongside.

Honors & Awards

Writing & Scholarship
NJ Hall of Fame Essay Contest — First Place, High School (2025)
Selected statewide by the NJ Hall of Fame and NJEA for an essay on Senator Andy Kim — how his election breaks barriers and redefines representation in New Jersey. Read the announcement ↗
Scholastic Art & Writing — Gold Key (2025)
“The Changing Face of Mental Health Narratives in Literature,” on how storytelling shapes public perception of mental illness.
Peace Islands Art & Essay — First Place, Essay (2025)
“You are Enough: The First Step to Peace” — honored by then-Congresswoman (now Governor) Mikie Sherrill. Read the essay ↗
College Board National Recognition (2025)
First-Generation Recognition Award for academic achievement.
Accepting first place at the Peace Islands Institute 15th Annual Art & Essay Contest (high school) — recognized with a U.S. House of Representatives citation from Rep. (now Gov.) Mikie Sherrill.
Music
WorldStrides Maestro Award (2026)
Outstanding solo cello performance; carries an invitation to perform at Carnegie Hall.
NY Youth Symphony Chamber Program (2025–26)
Selected among the program’s top 10 musicians to perform at Carnegie Hall.
VITAS International Music Competition — First Place (2026)
National recognition for classical performance.
Int’l Showcase Competition, Berlin — First Place (2024)
Performed at Laeiszhalle Recital Hall, Hamburg, Germany.
